name: Sourdough Bundt Cake with Buttermilk Glaze
description: Sourdough Bundt Cake with Buttermilk Glaze is a perfect snack cake. The tangy-sweet buttermilk glaze forms an ultra-thin coating over the melt-in-your-mouth cake.

author.name: Eileen Gray
author.description: Eileen Gray is a pastry chef, cookbook writer and the content creator behind Baking Sense. Eileen graduated from culinary school with high honors and was chosen class valedictorian. After graduation Eileen worked in fine dining restaurants, at a wholesale bakery, for a luxury hotel, at a retail bakery and even for a British tea shop. From 2007-2017 Eileen owned and operated an award-winning custom cake bakery (Cake Art Studio). Her cakes have been featured in regional and national cake decorating and bridal magazines.

publisher.name: Baking Sense®
publisher.description: The Art And Science of Baking

recipeIngredient.name: 8 oz sourdough discard (1 cup, room temperature)
9 oz cake flour (2 cups, see note)
6 oz unsalted butter (room temperature, cut into 1" chunks)

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Generously butter and flour a 12 cup Bundt pan.

itemListElement.text: Whisk together the eggs, yolks, vanilla and the discard, set aside.
Sift the flour, sugar, baking powder and salt into a mixer bowl. Mix on low speed to combine the dry ingredients. With the mixer running, toss the chunks of butter into the flour mixture.
Add the buttermilk and increase the speed to medium. Mix on medium high for 2 minutes to aerate the batter. Scrape the bowl and beater.
Add the egg mixture in 3 batches, scraping the bowl between each addition. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
Bake until the cake springs back when lightly pressed or a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 40 minutes.
Cool for 10 minutes in the pan. Invert the cake onto a cooling rack set over a clean sheet pan. Cool until slightly warm before glazing.
Combine the sugar, vanilla and buttermilk in a small bowl and whisk until smooth.
Pour the glaze over the still slightly warm cake. You can scoop up the glaze from the sheet pan and use it to fill in any gaps in the glaze or leave it with the drips.
Cool completely and allow the glaze to set. Transfer to a serving plate.
